overall_grade: Scientific evidence: exploratory / non-confirmatory. Critical review checks complete.
current_verdict: The solar-influence hypothesis is not confirmed. The analysis documents a robust high-specificity classification fact, weak sensitivity, weak null-model support, no identified global-SILSO year-FE causal estimate, non-confirmatory country-varying exposure tests, and no out-of-sample onset-prediction gain from solar exposure. Overall onset prediction changes after adding solar: delta AUC=-0.010162936594262595, delta log-loss=0.5292796652084675. Vulnerable-country onset prediction changes: delta AUC=-0.010397379290699282, delta log-loss=0.18425186240661884, shifted-exposure placebo p=0.5283018867924528.
hypothesis_verdict: Do not reject the skeptical null standard for causal solar influence on revolutions.

gates.label: RED source ingest and annual reconstruction
gates.evidence: 343 included RED episodes between 1900 and 2014; annual summary file: /api/red/annual-summary.json.
gates.implication: The paper-style RED annual comparison is reproducible from the local archive.

gates.label: SILSO solar input available
gates.evidence: Catalog contains climate.solar_activity.
gates.implication: Solar activity can be aligned with RED; absence would block the replication.

gates.label: Broad historical and macro-financial controls available
gates.evidence: 168 non-RED, non-SILSO catalog series are available for browsing/robustness screens.
gates.implication: The analysis can test whether the solar signal is fragile against more direct historical mechanisms.

gates.label: Sovereign-default and crisis controls available
gates.evidence: 9 Reinhart-Rogoff/HBS controls, 5 JST controls, and 10 Shiller market/rate controls are cataloged.
gates.implication: Debt default, crisis, market, and interest-rate channels are no longer an unmodeled omission at the global annual level.

gates.label: Mass protest and physical shock controls available
gates.evidence: 4 mass-mobilization controls and 3 volcanic sulfate forcing controls are cataloged.
gates.implication: The page can compare solar activity with protest and physical-shock inputs, not only financial variables.

gates.label: V-Dem, NAVCO, WDI, and food/agriculture inputs available
gates.evidence: 6 V-Dem regime/civil-society controls, 6 NAVCO resistance-event series, 11 World Bank WDI macro-development controls, and 6 food/agriculture stressor series are cataloged.
gates.implication: The next scientific question can be tested against stronger political-opportunity, mobilization, and political-economy alternatives instead of relying on RED/SILSO alone.

gates.label: Solar incremental value versus stronger alternatives screened
gates.evidence: RED episodes: SILSO r=0.20138791599212022, 26 non-solar controls stronger, solar delta R2 after top controls=0.02116154571498874. NAVCO events: SILSO r=-0.14832183768094137, 41 non-solar controls stronger, solar delta R2 after top controls=0.018425584425141195.
gates.implication: The broad science-review screen asks whether solar adds explanatory value beyond political-economy and mobilization alternatives; current values support caution rather than causal solar language.

gates.label: Exploratory astrological transit proxies available
gates.evidence: 6 synthetic astrological transit proxy series are cataloged as low-confidence contextual inputs.
gates.implication: These are available for fun/browsing and multiple-comparison stress testing, not as confirmatory mechanisms.

gates.label: RED geography exposed beyond global totals
gates.evidence: Region file: /api/red/annual-by-region.json; country file: /api/red/annual-by-country.json.
gates.implication: Country-year RED browsing is possible; this is necessary for panel diagnostics.

gates.label: Country-year panel diagnostic generated
gates.evidence: 159 countries, 115 years, 18285 country-years; dummy-FE event p≈0.014071540193820953; within-country FE year-clustered p≈0.06250834186996579.
gates.implication: The page now tests whether country-FE/trend panel estimates tell the same story as global annual correlations, using year-clustered uncertainty for the shared annual solar exposure.

gates.label: Identified country + year fixed-effect solar exposure
gates.evidence: 159 countries and 18285 country-years use country-varying solar radiation geometry x geomagnetic coupling x SILSO exposure; event p≈0.7611219244789675.
gates.implication: This directly addresses the main identification gap: country and year fixed effects can be included because exposure varies by country-year.

gates.label: Geomagnetic sensitivity and auroral-proximity exposure screen
gates.evidence: High-sensitivity stratum: countries=53, onsets=78, onset p=0.46291106306178154. Lower-sensitivity stratum: countries=129, onsets=253, onset p=0.8637529737563803. Exposure x high-sensitivity onset p=0.8219620586535878; auroral-proximity onset p=0.9186632549512836.
gates.implication: This tests the more geographically specific mechanism: if solar/geomagnetic effects matter, evidence should strengthen in high magnetic-latitude or auroral-proximity exposure screens.

gates.label: NASA POWER surface-radiation robustness exposure
gates.evidence: 159 countries and 4929 post-1984 country-years use NASA POWER ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN x geomagnetic coupling x SILSO exposure; event p≈0.3869712383595091.
gates.implication: This checks whether the identified country+year FE result survives replacing latitude radiation geometry with observed/reanalysis surface shortwave radiation where POWER coverage exists.

gates.label: Controlled country + year FE exposure screen
gates.evidence: 6 V-Dem/WDI country-year controls are merged into the two-way FE event model. Geometry exposure: n=578, countries=68, beta=0.0010255124449423895, p=0.9515551513769613. NASA POWER exposure: n=578, beta=0.009494525323564035, p=0.5752918759263802.
gates.implication: This directly addresses the country-year control concern; current controlled estimates do not support robust causal solar language.

gates.label: RED onset/event-history and out-of-sample prediction screen
gates.evidence: Onset risk set: 17950 country-years, 331 onsets, 9 active continuation years excluded. Controlled two-way FE onset p-values: geometry=0.9720610438455987, NASA POWER=0.6542646804102217. Out-of-sample geometry delta AUC=-0.010162936594262595, delta log-loss=0.5292796652084675, placebo p=0.25925925925925924. Vulnerable-country test: n=430, events=17, delta AUC=-0.010397379290699282, delta log-loss=0.18425186240661884, placebo p=0.5283018867924528. NASA POWER delta AUC=None.
gates.implication: This addresses the main event-history concern by modeling new revolution onset rather than active-year counts and asking whether solar exposure improves predictive validation beyond conventional controls.

gates.label: RED revolution-type heterogeneity screen
gates.evidence: 10 RED subtype onset screens were estimated from goal/form/outcome fields. Strongest unadjusted subtype=Civil-resistance revolutions; geometry p=0.20646531764133313; NASA POWER p=0.22249774296912517; onsets=10.
gates.implication: This tests whether any solar association is concentrated in theoretically distinct revolution types. Subtype findings remain exploratory because many subgroup tests are screened.

gates.label: Lag, vulnerability-interaction, quantile, and placebo-timing screens explored
gates.implication: The stronger-mechanism search found no robust lag or timing evidence. The GDP-growth interaction is exploratory and multiple-comparison vulnerable, so it is a candidate for preregistered follow-up rather than evidence of confirmation.

gates.label: Time-structured null model included
gates.evidence: 114 circular solar-year shifts; two-sided p=0.2.
gates.implication: This is stronger than naive independent-year p-values, though still not a causal identification strategy.

gates.label: Primary high-revolution specificity endpoint audited
gates.evidence: Exact solar-maximum/top-quartile RED endpoint specificity=0.9358974358974359; balanced accuracy=0.5355162855162855; first-half specificity=0.9302325581395349; second-half specificity=0.9428571428571428; leave-one-cycle-out specificity range=0.927536231884058 to 0.9436619718309859; TP/FP/FN/TN=5/5/32/73; permutation specificity p=0.1888111888111888; random-window specificity p=0.19880119880119881; circular specificity p=0.1826086956521739.
gates.implication: This supports a narrow descriptive classification result. It does not, by itself, establish prediction quality or causal influence.

gates.label: Preregistered threshold grid and train/test quantization screen
gates.evidence: 24 predeclared quantile/window rules are reported. First-half-selected rule: q=0.9, threshold=5.0, radius=1; held-out specificity=0.8181818181818182, sensitivity=0.2857142857142857, balanced accuracy=0.551948051948052. Fixed primary p75/exact held-out specificity=0.95, sensitivity=0.07894736842105263, balanced accuracy=0.5144736842105263.
gates.implication: This directly addresses threshold cherry-picking risk by showing the whole quantization grid and evaluating a threshold chosen on earlier years against later years.

gates.label: Visual overlay tested with shape-aware synchrony diagnostics
gates.evidence: 5-year smoothed Pearson=0.1558000792039528; circular-shift p=0.46956521739130436; best lag=8y with r=0.2818857498735187; peak-distance null p=0.5344655344655345.
gates.implication: The graph overlay is now quantified with smoothed co-movement, lag search, peak proximity, phase counts, and event-study diagnostics; current values should be read as visual-context checks, not confirmatory synchrony.

gates.label: Monthly RED onset timing and lagged SILSO screen
gates.evidence: 343 RED episodes have known start months; strongest monthly lag=3 months with r=0.06143324426133216 and circular-shift p=0.289760348583878.
gates.implication: This addresses timing sensitivity beyond annual aggregation. Current monthly timing must beat circular-shift SILSO timing before annual overlays can be read as mechanistic evidence.

gates.label: Pre-analysis/specification registry published
gates.evidence: Registry declares primary outcome, exposure, nulls, robustness checks, controls, multiple-comparison policy, and causal-identification rule.
gates.implication: Readers can distinguish confirmatory requirements from exploratory browsing and post-hoc model selection.

gates.label: Global SILSO non-identification under country + year fixed effects documented
gates.evidence: Solar activity is identical for every country in a given year, so year fixed effects absorb all solar variation. The page therefore reports identified country-varying solar-exposure models instead of pretending plain global SILSO can be estimated with year fixed effects.
gates.implication: The review now treats the year-FE absorption problem as an explicit identification constraint and uses country-varying exposure as the estimable robustness design.

gates.label: Causal/predictive claim appropriately constrained
gates.evidence: The implemented diagnostics still rely on observational annual alignment; remaining confounding and model-selection risk are material, so the page states exploratory association/stress test rather than causal confirmation.
gates.implication: This is a scientific limitation, not a positive result for the hypothesis: the public conclusion is limited to what the evidence can support.
